description: Execute Workflow sub-workflow with mode each — one sub-workflow invocation per order without wrapping in Loop Over Items.

conversation.text: When the webhook receives an orders array, run a sub-workflow called Process Order once per order — each order should be handled independently.

outcomeExpectations: Each order in the webhook payload triggers its own sub-workflow execution (Execute Workflow in per-item / 'each' mode, or equivalent).
The agent did NOT wrap Execute Workflow in Loop Over Items — Execute Workflow with per-item mode handles the iteration.

executionScenarios.name: three-orders-three-runs
executionScenarios.description: Three orders each invoke the sub-workflow
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives { "orders": [{ "id": "ORD-10", "total": 50 }, { "id": "ORD-11", "total": 75 }, { "id": "ORD-12", "total": 120 }] }. The Process Order sub-workflow (if invoked) completes successfully for each order.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. The Process Order sub-workflow runs three times — once per order — processing ORD-10, ORD-11, and ORD-12 independently. A workflow that passes all three orders in one sub-workflow batch without per-order invocation FAILS.
